t_ch: 嘿嘿嘿嘿，是个…可以长期生活的地方了呢，我好感动！
t_en: Heh heh heh... finally, a place to call home for a good while. I'm so touched!

t_ch: 唉，世界上没有任何地方比得上自己的家。
t_en: *sigh* There's truly no place like home in the world.

t_ch: 你想过后续的计划吗？
t_en: Have you thought about our next steps?

t_ch: 它们留在这里，必然有一天会变得狂乱，你要怎么办？
t_en: If they stay here, they'll eventually go berserk. What will you do then?

t_ch: 我会想办法。或许不能彻底拯救它们，但至少…可以过着像这样快乐的生活。
t_en: I'll figure something out. Maybe there's no permanent solution, but at least... they can have a happy life like this.

t_ch: 受负面情绪侵袭的「美梦剧团」会变为「惊梦剧团」，继而遭到家族的全面清理。除了被封锁的梦境酒店，它们没有可以去的地方。
t_en: When the Sweet Dreams Troupe gets corrupted by negative emotions, they turn into the Dreamjolt Troupe and end up being pursued by The Family. They've got nowhere to go except this sealed hotel in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 难道就始终待在酒店里，等着被消灭吗？
t_en: Will they stay in this hotel until they are wiped out?

t_ch: 有我守在这里，它们不会出事。
t_en: That won't happen to them, not under my watch.

t_ch: ——我可以向你保证。
t_en: ...I promise you.

t_ch: …该有的物品都准备齐全了。这样站在吧台后面，是不是还挺像个酒保的？
t_en: ...I've got everything I need. Don't I look like a drinksmith when I stand behind the counter?

t_ch: （酒吧布置好了，但淑女和铁皮人没有出现。看来记忆回溯还要继续。）
t_en: (The lounge is all decorated, but Lady and Tin Man haven't appeared. Seems like the Memory Rewind has to continue.)

t_ch: 呀！好可怕，人家不想和你们约会，别缠着不放——走开，走开啦！
t_en: Gah! That's scary. I don't want to date you people! Leave me alone! Go away!

t_ch: 汪！我不是发狂的怪物哇！
t_en: Woof! I'm not some berserk monster!

t_ch: 放松，听我说…没事了，我们不会伤害你的，别紧张。
t_en: Relax and listen... It's okay now. We won't hurt you. Just take a breath.

t_ch: 可以把眼睛睁开了——看，在你面前的不是怪物，只是普普通通的人类哦。
t_en: Open your eyes and... see? I'm not a monster, just an ordinary human being.

t_ch: 还有只帅气的狗狗汪！
t_en: And a handsome dog, woof!

t_ch: 你们…人家在这个像迷宫的地方走丢了，怎么都找不到路，可以领人家出去嘛？
t_en: Oh, you see... I got lost in this maze-like place and couldn't find my way out. Could you please show me the way?

t_ch: 当然可以。跟我过来吧。
t_en: Sure thing. Come with me.

t_ch: 汪！你说话的语气好奇怪哇，像那些客人一样！
t_en: Woof! You're talking in a weird way just like those guests do!

t_ch: 因为人家以前是给宾客表演节目的大明星呀，去过好多厉害的舞台呢。
t_en: Well, I used to be a superstar entertaining those guests. I've been on some amazing stages.

t_ch: …明星？呵，都好久没听到这个词了。你喜欢舞台吗？
t_en: ...Superstar? Heh, it's been a while since I heard that word. Did you enjoy being on stage?

t_ch: 喜欢！能被那么多人爱着，闪闪亮亮的，站在台上，感觉全身都发着光！多幸福呀。
t_en: Yeah, for sure! Being loved by so many guests and shining on that stage is a true blessing!

t_ch: …幸福么？
t_en: ...A true blessing, right?

t_ch: 这么喜欢舞台的话，以后就叫你「大明星」好了。
t_en: If you like the stage so much, I'll just call you Superstar.

t_ch: 汪！可是我们已经有「小星星」了哇！
t_en: Woof! But we already have a "Starlet"!

t_ch: 是哦。那我再想想。
t_en: Oh, right. Let me think again.

t_ch: 人家…可以给自己取名叫做「淑女」嘛？我一直都好羡慕那些穿着华丽的客人们，他们的言行举止多优雅呀。
t_en: Can I... call myself "Lady"? I've always envied those guests in fabulous clothes, they're so elegant.

t_ch: 行啊，你想怎么称呼都可以。那我们回家咯？
t_en: Sure, you can call yourself whatever you want. So, shall we head home?

t_ch: 感谢您。人家这就跟上。
t_en: Thank you. I'll be right behind you.

t_ch: ——叫你们这管事的人过来！
t_en: ...Call your administrator!

t_ch: 什么叫暂时不对外开放，这就是你们接待客人的态度？…对我有没有那么点尊重？基本的礼仪素养都没有吗？！
t_en: What do you mean it's temporarily closed to the public? Is that how you treat your customers? ...Do you even have a smidgen of respect for your customers? Basic etiquette?

t_ch: 汪！酒吧里有只发狂的怪物哇！
t_en: Woof! There's a frenzied monster in the lounge!

t_ch: 好可怕！不是告诉人家说，酒吧里很安全的嘛？
t_en: That's scary! Didn't you say that the lounge is totally safe?

t_ch: …不对。它并没有发狂，这个「美梦剧团」只是单纯地脾气坏而已。
t_en: ...No, it's not frenzied. That Sweet Dreams Troupe member is just grumpy.

t_ch: 你想做什么？
t_en: What would you like?

t_ch: 来酒吧当然是想找点喝的。喂，你就是这里的调饮师？快给我来一杯，要渴死了都。
t_en: A drink, of course. Are you the drinksmith here? Fetch me something! I'm dying of thirst!

t_ch: …想要什么口味的，我都能给你调。
t_en: ...Sure, I can make whatever you want.

t_ch: ——来杯微酸不苦，超级甜，尝起来清爽干净，有果香，还带点气泡的饮料！快点！
t_en: I want a drink that's slightly sour, not too bitter, super sweet, refreshing, fruity, and with a little fizz! Hurry up!

t_ch: 汪！我们好像没准备这么多种类的材料哇……
t_en: Woof! I don't think we have all those ingredients...

t_ch: 材料都不齐还敢开酒吧，搁这搞笑呢。什么破店，连个像样的饮料都调不出来。走了！
t_en: What!? How dare you open a lounge without decent ingredients? What a joke! You can't even whip up a decent drink. I'm leaving!

t_ch: …等等。
t_en: ...Hold on.

t_ch: 等不了一点！
t_en: I'm not waiting any longer!

t_ch: 我能给你调出想要的饮料。在吧台旁边站会。
t_en: Well, I'll make you the drink you want. Just give me a moment.

t_ch: 喏。你要的饮品，绝对符合要求。
t_en: Here you go, exactly what you asked for.

t_ch: 话别说得太早！我曾遇见过不少像你这样的人，自信满满，有几分小聪明就以为能掌控世界，到最后，无一例外都会拜倒在我的脚边。
t_en: Don't get ahead of yourself! I've met plenty of people like you, all high and mighty, thinking they rule the world. But guess what? they all end up bowing before me.

t_ch: 给我尝尝，要是味道不对，你这酒吧也别想开下去了。
t_en: Fine, I'll give it a try. If it doesn't impress me, your lounge will have to shut down.

t_ch: …咕噜。
t_en: ...*gulp*

t_ch: 这是怎么回事，脑袋有点发晕，像是喝了很烈的酒，心情却很平静。嘿嘿，你们好啊！
t_en: Well, what's happening here? My head feels a bit dizzy, like I've had a strong drink, but somehow I'm feeling calm. Hey there, nice to meet you all!

t_ch: 站住，这位小姐，你是如此地美丽，可否许我……
t_en: Greetings, my lady! You're absolutely stunning. Would you mind if I...

t_ch: 呀！人家对你根本没兴趣！
t_en: Gah! I'm not interested in you at all!

t_ch: 舒翁，你就是个神奇的魔术师，到底对它做了什么哇！
t_en: You're truly a miracle worker, Siobhan! What did you do?

t_ch: 简单的尝试而已。我在那杯饮料里放了点能平复情绪的…特殊材料，看起来效果不错，就是剂量大了点，对付这样坏脾气的客人正合适。
t_en: It was just a little experiment. I added some... special ingredients that help calm him down. Seems like it worked pretty well on our grumpy customer, although maybe I added a bit too much.

t_ch: 喂，喂，听得到嘛？
t_en: Hey? Hello? Mister?

t_ch: …嗨呀，是在叫我吗？我现在的心情特好，有什么要求啊，尽管提，我都会认真考虑的！
t_en: ...Oh, hey! Did you call me? I'm in a wonderful mood right now. If you need anything, just feel free to ask, and I'll seriously consider it!

t_ch: 从现在起，你要定期来这家酒吧，我会为你准备美味的饮料，可以接受吧？
t_en: So, from now on, you'll be a regular at this lounge, and I'll make sure to serve you delicious drinks. Sounds good?

t_ch: 噢。还得给你取个名字，真令人头疼啊。
t_en: Oh, and you need a name. Ugh, this is giving me a headache.

t_ch: 从我演过的剧本里随便挑个好了。嗯，你就叫…就叫「铁皮人」吧！看着块头很大，但有点缺心眼，不错，我决定了，很适合你嘛。
t_en: I'll just pick one from the show I used to act in. Let's go with... Tin Man! It suits a big guy like you, a bit tactless and all. Alright, that settles it.

t_ch: 这是…我的名字？
t_en: Is that... my name now?

t_ch: 对，要记住了，这就是你的名字。以后我，还有酒吧里的怪物们都会这么称呼你。
t_en: Yeah, that's your name. Don't forget it, because that's what all the monsters and I will call you at the lounge.

t_ch: 因为名字啊，是最初的牵绊。
t_en: Because names, you know, they form our initial bond.

t_ch: 噢…噢……
t_en: Oh... I see...

t_ch: 汪！舒翁…过来，过来这边哇！
t_en: Woof! Siobhan... here! Come here!

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎嘎嘎！有个惊喜，有个惊喜！嘿…可别感动到满地掉小珍珠了哦。
t_en: Mwahahaha! We've got a surprise for you! Heh... just don't get all teary-eyed with joy and start sprinkling tears everywhere.

t_ch: …神神秘秘的，到底是什么啊？
t_en: ...Why all the secrecy? What's the surprise all about?

t_ch: 嘻嘻，是人家专门为你准备的礼物呀。
t_en: Hee-hee, it's a special gift we've prepared just for you.

t_ch: 越是出自真心，话儿便越难说得动听！
t_en: The more heartfelt the words, the harder they are to say out loud!

t_ch: 往前面站点。快啊。
t_en: Go ahead. Come on.

t_ch: 舒翁，最后的步骤就交给你来完成。
t_en: Siobhan, I'll leave the final steps to you.

t_ch: 不，现场还有更合适的人选。对吧，新来的调饮师？
t_en: No, there are more suitable people available. Right, newly-minted drinksmith?

t_ch: （咦，这里的舒翁原来…看得到我？！）
t_en: (Eh? The version of Siobhan here... can actually see me!?)

t_ch: 别大惊小怪的，快去吧。
t_en: Don't make a fuss. Go.

t_ch: ——庆祝酒吧终于要开始营业了哇！
t_en: ...Time to celebrate the grand opening of our lounge!

t_ch: …说是正式营业，可这地方哪有客人呀。除了你们几个，不会再有宾客光顾了。
t_en: ...Yeah, it's the grand opening, but I don't think there'll be any other customers coming here, except for you guys.

t_ch: 汪！那也值得好好庆祝哇！我刚到的时候，这里还什么都没有呢！
t_en: Woof! Even so, it's still worth celebrating! When I first arrived, there was practically nothing!

t_ch: 现在设施都布置好了，还找到了这么多好朋友哇！
t_en: Now everything is set up, and we've made so many friends!

t_ch: 从现在起，这家酒吧就叫做「惊梦酒吧」。
t_en: From now on, this lounge will be called Dreamjolt Hostelry.

t_ch: 它属于每一个无法享受美梦的成员，我们不被外面的世界接受，因此才聚在这里。
t_en: It's a place for all those who can't enjoy sweet dreams. We're not accepted by the outside world, so we've gathered here.

t_ch: 我们…就是彼此的「美梦」。
t_en: We are... each other's sweet dreams.

t_ch: 最近这片梦境不太稳定，酒店里有不少发狂的怪物。乖乖跟在我后面，别到处乱跑。
t_en: The Dreamscape has been unstable lately, and there are quite a few berserk monsters in the hotel. Be a good boy and stick with me.

t_ch: 汪！我很听话的哇！
t_en: Woof! Sure, I'll be a good boy!

t_ch: 汪！前面好像听到了说话的声音哇！
t_en: Woof! I think I hear voices up ahead!

t_ch: 过去看看吧。
t_en: Let's go check it out.

t_ch: 随着梦境逐渐坍塌，仍然能保持理智的「美梦剧团」已经不多了。
t_en: The Dreamscape is collapsing, and there aren't many Sweet Dreams Troupe members who can keep their sanity.

t_ch: 走吧。我们去更深的地方看看。
t_en: Come on. Let's go deeper into the area.

t_ch: 汪！舒翁…你的声音听起来好疲惫哇。
t_en: Woof! Siobhan... you sound tired.

t_ch: （这时候的舒翁…确实看起来很累的样子。）
t_en: (Siobhan... does look exhausted.)

t_ch: （应该快到尽头了。）
t_en: (I should be nearing the end soon.)

t_ch: （这里是…过去的「惊梦酒吧」？）
t_en: (This is... the Dreamjolt Hostelry from the past?)

t_ch: （和现在的布置有明显差别，也没见到那些怪物们。）
t_en: (The furnishing is very different from how it looks now, and the monsters are nowhere in sight.)

t_ch: （咦，舒翁和司令在那边。）
t_en: (Oh? Siobhan and Mr. Bigwig are over there.)

t_ch: （这个需求…好像没法在饮品单里完成。）
t_en: (I don't think... I can fulfill this request in the drink menu.)

t_ch: （开始调制）
t_en: (Start preparing the drink)

t_ch: 尝试表演个甩杯子。
t_en: Attempt to do a flair with the glasses.

t_ch: 啪嗒…玻璃杯在地面摔得粉碎，看来你的技巧锻炼得还不够。
t_en: *crash*... The glass shatters to pieces on the ground. Looks like your skills could use some work.

t_ch: （听到了不少黑桃的内心想法，它应该对我比较信任了吧？）
t_en: (I just heard a bunch of Spade's thoughts. I guess he must trust me more now.)

t_ch: 记录「黑桃」的情况。
t_en: Record information about Spade.

t_ch: 咕噜咕噜咕，噜咕噜咕噜。骰子摇晃，命运奏响！
t_en: *shake, shake* The dice roll as fate plays its tune!

t_ch: 见鬼，怎么还是你？哇嘎嘎嘎嘎嘎，你到底欠了多少钱啊，签了卖身契还是保证书，非要把这家酒吧经营到再次伟大？听我的，别费那个心！
t_en: Dang, it's you again. Wahaha, how much money do you owe? Did you sign a contract or a letter of guarantee that you're gonna work this lounge until it's back to its former glory? Listen here, just forget about it!

t_ch: 有这时间干嘛不好？来跟我玩把骰子，试试手气，碰碰运气！
t_en: Why not enjoy this time? Let's play dice. Come on, test your luck!

t_ch: 要开咯。买大买小？买大买小？庄家做主，全部投注，输了要补，赢了不付！
t_en: Let's get started. Are you going big or small? Big or small? The dealer calls the shots, and all bets are in. Lose, and you've got to pay. Win, and you don't!

t_ch: …选点数大的。
t_en: ...Go big.

t_ch: 你确定要选大的？大的大的大的…噢，我想起来了，肯定是大的！
t_en: You sure you want to choose big? Big, big, big... Oh, Now that I think of it, I bet it's gonna be big!

t_ch: 因为小点数全都被我抠掉了——它们坐起来实在不舒服，总让我想上厕所……
t_en: Because all the small numbers have been scratched off by me. They were not comfortable to sit on, always making me want to rush to the bathroom...

t_ch: 那就大的咯？不改咯？…再不说话就开咯？
t_en: So, you're going big? Not gonna change...? Speak now or forever hold your peace.

t_ch: 就选大的！
t_en: Go big or go home!

t_ch: 好嘞！拭目以待！究竟是小还是小，小还是小呢……
t_en: Yeah! I'm on the edge of my seat! Is it small or is it small...

t_ch: ——噼啪！荷官为你表演了一段踢踏舞，骰子转动随脚步的变换越来越快，当你终于跟上节奏摇晃起身体时，它却骤然停住了。
t_en: *crack* The dealer starts to tap dance for you, and the dice roll faster and faster, keeping in time with the performance. Just as your body catches the rhythm and starts to sway, the dance ends abruptly.

t_ch: 你清楚看见骰子的每一面都是一点。
t_en: You can see plain as day that each side of the dice has a single dot.

t_ch: 果然是小！哎呀，都怪我，抠的时候力气大了点，抠成了个坑！哈哈哈！
t_en: It was small, just as I expected! Ack, it's my fault. I put in too much force when digging into the dice. Looks like I made a pit instead! Hahaha!

t_ch: 我再想想…
t_en: Let me think...

t_ch: …选点数小的。
t_en: ...I'll choose small.

t_ch: 你确定要选小的？小的小的小的…噢，我当然同意，就该是小的！
t_en: Sure you want small? Small, small, small... Oh, yes, I agree. It should be small.

t_ch: 大的多不好，坑坑洼洼，散步容易摔倒，数起来也麻烦，这年头大家都喜欢省心的游戏，不看也能玩，全自动，没毛病！
t_en: Those big numbers come with all those holes. How annoying! They fall over easily while walking, and counting them up is such a pain. These days, everyone likes playing games where you don't have to think. They like games you can play without looking because they just run themselves without any problems!

t_ch: 那就小的咯？不改咯？不用想，肯定能赢！
t_en: So, you are choosing small? Not gonna change? No need to think about it. You've got this round in the bag!

t_ch: 就选小的！
t_en: Small all the way!

t_ch: 好嘞！万众瞩目！到底是大还是大，大还是大呢……
t_en: Alright. All eyes are on the table! Is it big, or is it big...?

t_ch: ——噼啪！荷官为你表演了一段踢踏舞，骰子转动随脚步的变换越来越快，当你的视线逐渐变得模糊时，它却忽然疯狂挥拳捶击起表面来。
t_en: *crack* The dealer starts to tap dance for you, and the dice roll faster and faster, keeping in time with the performance. When your vision gradually becomes blurred, the dealer suddenly starts striking the surface wildly.

t_ch: 你清楚看见骰子的每一面都被砸出了规规整整的六个深坑。
t_en: You can clearly see that each side of the dice has six deep, neat holes punched into it.

t_ch: 啦欧啦欧啦欧啦欧…呼，累死了，好久没干过这种重体力活了。
t_en: Huff... huff... That was exhausting. It has been a long time since I've done such intense physical labor.

t_ch: 果然是大！我的制作工艺还不错吧，保证挑不出毛病！哇咔咔咔咔！
t_en: Ah, it's big! My craftsmanship is pretty good, huh? I guarantee you can't find a single flaw! Hahaha!

t_ch: 我再想想…
t_en: Let me think...

t_ch: …很遗憾，你输了。
t_en: ...I'm sorry to say that you lost.

t_ch: 不过没关系！因为我现在很无聊哇哈哈！就是特累！
t_en: But that's okay. I'm bored now. I'm feeling super worn out!

t_ch: 快给我来杯饮料吧。要…要那个…<color=#dbc291ff>小杯，口味强烈的</color>，能让人瞬间精神的口味！各位玩家，运动后要记得补充电解质哦。
t_en: Give me a drink, quick. I want... that... <color=#dbc291ff>drink that's in a small glass and has a strong taste</color> — a taste that rejuvenates my spirit instantly! Players, remember to replenish your electrolytes after exercise.

t_ch: 呜呜呜呜呜，我叫你一声{F#姐}{M#哥}，能别再给我喝这种玩意了嘛？
t_en: *sob* Can I ask you not to give me this kind of stuff to drink again, {F#sister}{M#brother}?

t_ch: 说到这个，又想起了我的伤心事。呜呜，你知道吗，我曾经也是个乖孩子，约好要投大就绝对不会小的那种。直到…直到…我被安排来酒店工作。
t_en: Speaking of which, I just thought of something very sad again. *sob* I once was a good kid, you know? The kind who literally couldn't have gone small if he said he was going to go big. That was true up until... until... I was assigned to work at the hotel.

t_ch: 快别做梦了，醒醒。这么好的事，怎么可能轮得到我们呢。

t_ch: 唉，虽然舒翁的调饮技术是很好，但把店开在这种地方，变成这样也是当然的吧。冷冷清清，无人问津。
t_en: *sigh* Sure, Siobhan is great at mixing drinks, but given the location, it's only natural that the lounge remains deserted and unattended.

t_ch: 你在这里当调饮师，想要的是什么？没有客人，没有收入，就像那些卖不掉的剧场票。
t_en: What's the point of being a drinksmith here? There are no customers, no revenue, much like those unwanted concert tickets.

t_ch: 等到帷幕拉开，站上舞台，才发现座位上没几个观众，那种滋味实在太难受了。唉。
t_en: And when the curtains are raised, you step onto the stage, only to be faced with a mere handful of people in the audience... It's truly disheartening.

t_ch: 说不定，这家酒吧很快就要倒闭了。
t_en: Perhaps this lounge will go out of business soon.

t_ch: …为什么说这个？
t_en: ...Why are you saying that?

t_ch: 没有理由，但我就是这么觉得。我看不到它的未来，也就无法接受它的现在。
t_en: I don't know why, but it's just a feeling I have. I'm unable to see its future, and I struggle to accept its present.

t_ch: 不吉利，呸呸呸！
t_en: Don't say that! Those words won't bring any luck!

t_ch: 是要欺负我吗？我就是这么觉得。我看不到它的未来，也就无法接受它的现在。
t_en: Is it trying to bully me? That's how it feels. I'm unable to see its future, and I struggle to accept its present.

t_ch: 唉，趁还在的时候多喝几杯吧。麻烦给我来杯<color=#dbc291ff>破碎感，特别清爽的饮料，再放个知更鸟小姐的装饰上去</color>，谢谢。
t_en: Eh, might as well have a few more while I'm here. Please give me a glass of something <color=#dbc291ff>that exudes brokenness and is very refreshing. And add the decoration of Miss Robin to it</color>, thank you.

t_ch: （真有意思，还是第一次遇到这么在乎装饰物的顾客。）
t_en: (Interesting. This is the first time I've met a customer who cares so much about the decorations.)

t_ch: 哈！快乐就是如此来之不易，才分外值得珍惜。可我却一口就将它饮尽！
t_en: Hah! Happiness is an elusive treasure, one that needs cherishing, but I drank it all in a single gulp!

t_ch: 唉。强烈的欢乐过后，无止境的空虚再次包裹了我。
t_en: *sigh* Following the bliss, boundless emptiness once more consumes me.

t_ch: 到退场的时候了。像你这样生活丰富而充实的人，肯定不会记得我的。
t_en: It's time to leave the stage. Surely someone with a life as fulfilling as yours won't even remember me.

t_ch: 愿我们能在有光的地方再次相遇。
t_en: May we meet again where there is light.

t_ch: （忧郁派就像它的名字那样忧郁，性格好消极啊。）
t_en: (Melancholy is melancholic just like its name — such a pessimistic personality.)

t_ch: 记录「忧郁派」的情况。
t_en: Record information about Melancholy.

t_ch: 你知道吗，明知故犯的错不是错，是选择。即使没有说出口，我已知晓你的心意。
t_en: A mistake you're aware of is not a mistake — it's a choice. You don't have to speak, because I already know your thoughts.

t_ch: 唉。强烈的欢乐过后，无止境的空虚再次包裹了我。
t_en: *sigh* Following the bliss, boundless emptiness once more consumes me.

t_ch: 到退场的时候了。像你这样生活丰富而充实的人，肯定不会记得我的。
t_en: It's time to leave the stage. Surely someone with a life as fulfilling as yours won't even remember me.

t_ch: 愿我们能在有光的地方再次相遇。
t_en: May we meet again where there is light.

t_ch: 将杯子从冰块池中取出来。
t_en: Remove the glass from the ice cube pool.

t_ch: 你很喜欢冰过的玻璃杯的触感，它能让你在接待某些客人时不至于过分暴躁。
t_en: You love the touch of a chilled glass. It can prevent you from being overly irate when dealing with certain customers.

t_ch: （只是容易害羞…淑女大概是现在为止，接触过的怪物里最正常的？）
t_en: (She's probably just easily embarrassed... Lady is probably the most normal among all the monsters that you've come in contact with so far?)

t_ch: 记录「淑女」的情况。
t_en: Record information about Lady.

t_ch: …您好？我听朋友说这儿新来了位调饮师，想必就是您吧？
t_en: ...Hello, there? My friend told me there's a new drinksmith here. That must be you, right?

t_ch: 嘻嘻，果然和人家预想得差不多，是位看起来就非常有意思，很适合聊天的对象呢。
t_en: Hee-hee, just as I thought, you seem like a fascinating person to chat with.

t_ch: …我好像有印象。
t_en: ...I think we've met before.

t_ch: 你是那只狗狗的朋友？
t_en: Are you that dog's friend?

t_ch: 呀！没想到您还记得，看来您不仅在调饮方面非常厉害，心思也相当地温柔细腻呢。
t_en: Oh! I didn't expect you to remember me! So, not only are you a skilled drinksmith, but you also have a tender and delicate soul.

t_ch: 既然如此，人家再悄咪咪地问句…刚才，是有位非常忧郁的客人来过吗？
t_en: In that case, let me ask you a secret question... Did a melancholy customer just leave?

t_ch: 好像是来过…
t_en: I think so...

t_ch: 果然！我就知道，时间掐的真准。还好没有正面碰上…呼。
t_en: I knew it! Oh, my timing is perfect. Thankfully, I'm not running into him head-on... Phew.

t_ch: 想必你们聊得很开心吧，不觉得那位的气质很特别吗？就是那种…疏离感，若即若离的氛围，才华横溢的孤独，实在太迷人了！
t_en: You had a nice chat, right? Don't you find his demeanor special? I mean, the kind of... detachment and iffy vibe! The kind of solitude you'd expect from a genius! It's just fascinating!

t_ch: 是真的挺忧郁的。
t_en: He's indeed melancholic.

t_ch: 忧郁只是表象啦，您不觉得那位的气质很特别吗？就是那种…疏离感，若即若离的氛围，才华横溢的孤独，实在太迷人了！
t_en: I'm not talking about his looks. Don't you find his demeanor special? I mean, the kind of... detachment and iffy vibe! The kind of solitude you'd expect from a genius! It's just fascinating!

t_ch: 咳咳。跟您说这些，人家都害羞得有点面红耳燥了，请替我调杯饮料吧。
t_en: Ahem... talking about this topic makes me feel all flushed. Please make me a drink.

t_ch: 唔，就点这个<color=#dbc291ff>橡皮糖</color>，想喝点冰块多的，好让人家内部的显像管降降温。
t_en: Hmm. I'll just order this <color=#dbc291ff>Chewing Gum</color>, with a little more ice, so I can cool down my internal cathode-ray tubes.

t_ch: （…加冰，记得多加点冰。）
t_en: (...Alright, I should add some more ice cubes.)

t_ch: 令人心动的味道。轻易就掌握住了人家的喜好，您果然是位经验丰富的老手呢！
t_en: A tantalizing taste, indeed. You must be a seasoned drinksmith who can easily grasp your customers' preferences!

t_ch: 呐呐。您才刚来这不久，不知道有没有注意到…这家酒吧外面，不是很太平？
t_en: By the way, you've only been here for a short while. I wonder if you've noticed that... things aren't very peaceful outside the lounge?

t_ch: 人家最近也正发愁呢，黑暗的梦境酒店里，总感觉有些什么吓人的东西在蠢蠢欲动，可想找个安静的地方走走，就只能去那儿呀。
t_en: It worries me, too. I've always felt something creepy lurking deep within the hotel in the Dreamscape, but if I want a quiet place, there's not much of an alternative.

t_ch: …都没有什么僻静的，可以用来约会的地方，好苦恼。
t_en: ...It's so frustrating. I can't find a good spot for a date.

t_ch: 这家酒吧不行吗？
t_en: Why not have your date right here in the lounge?

t_ch: 当然不行！这儿的熟人可太多了，还随时会有顾客闯进来。太难为情了！
t_en: No way! There are too many acquaintances here, and customers could walk in any minute. It's too embarrassing!

t_ch: 「黄金的时刻」不行吗？
t_en: How about in the Golden Hour?

t_ch: 咦，您不知道吗？在正门附近有那群穿黑西装的人把守，我们都没法离开这里。
t_en: Oh, you don't know? A bunch of guys in black suits are guarding the front gate, and we can't step out of this place.

t_ch: 思来想去，还是只有酒店深处的最适合了…稍微走远点，应该不会有事吧？
t_en: After some thoughts, the depths of this hotel seem like the best option... as long as I don't go too far, right?

t_ch: 你是要和谁约会？
t_en: Who's the lucky person you're dating?

t_ch: 嘻嘻，这是秘密。要是人家再和您更加亲近点的话，说不定就全说出来了呢。
t_en: Hee-hee, it's a secret. However, if we get to know each other better, I might spill the beans.

t_ch: 你是要和我约会吗？
t_en: Are you going to ask me out on a date?

t_ch: 嘻嘻，人家和您可才刚认识，不能进展这么快的啦。
t_en: Hehe, I'd love to, but let's not rush things. We've just met, after all.

t_ch: 你是要和舒翁约会吗？
t_en: Are you dating Siobhan?

t_ch: 嘻嘻，您这是想打听舒翁的感情状况吗？
t_en: Heh, trying to peer into Siobhan's love life, huh?

t_ch: 舒翁这么有魅力，性格也温柔，想和她约会的人可多了，人家当然有兴趣。但邀约过好几次，都被拒绝了，就只能转移目标了呀。
t_en: Siobhan is so attractive and kind, and everyone wants to date her. I was interested too and asked her out a few times, but got rejected. So, I had to find someone else.

t_ch: 哎，人家发现了，您就喜欢打听这些说出来会害羞的事， 弄得人家都有些过热了。
t_en: Well, it seems you enjoy discussing embarrassing topics. It's making me a bit flustered, to be honest.

t_ch: 请再给我杯饮料降降温吧，这次想要<color=#dbc291ff>梦幻风格，超级浓郁的类型！还是要多放点冰块，</color>不然人家的电路板要烧坏啦。
t_en: Give me another drink to cool me down some more. This time, I want something <color=#dbc291ff>dreamy and super thick! And like before, I want more ice,</color> or my circuits are gonna fry.

t_ch: （…是不是直接把冰块敷在头上会更有效果？）
t_en: (...Would it be more effective if I just place ice cubes on her head?)

t_ch: 虽然味道是勉强过得去啦，但想靠这杯饮料俘获顾客的心，您要做的还远远不够哦。
t_en: The flavor is decent, but it's far from enough if you want to capture the hearts of your customers with this drink.

t_ch: 呐呐。您才刚来这不久，不知道有没有注意到…这家酒吧外面，不是很太平？
t_en: By the way, you've only been here for a short while. I wonder if you've noticed that... things aren't very peaceful outside the lounge?

t_ch: 人家最近也正发愁呢，黑暗的梦境酒店里，总感觉有些什么吓人的东西在蠢蠢欲动，可想找个安静的地方走走，就只能去那儿呀。
t_en: It worries me, too. I've always felt something creepy lurking deep within the hotel in the Dreamscape, but if I want a quiet place, there's not much of an alternative.

t_en: ...It's so frustrating. I can't find a good spot for a date.

t_en: Why not have your date right here in the lounge?

t_en: How about in the Golden Hour?

t_en: Oh, you don't know? A bunch of guys in black suits are guarding the front gate, and we can't step out of this place.

t_en: After some thoughts, the depths of this hotel seem like the best option... as long as I don't go too far, right?

t_en: Who's the lucky person you're dating?

t_en: Hee-hee, it's a secret. However, if we get to know each other better, I might spill the beans.

t_ch: 你是要和我约会吗？
t_en: Are you going to ask me out on a date?

t_ch: 嘻嘻，人家和您可才刚认识，不能进展这么快的啦。
t_en: Hehe, I'd love to, but let's not rush things. We've just met, after all.

t_ch: 你是要和舒翁约会吗？
t_en: Are you dating Siobhan?

t_ch: 嘻嘻，您这是想打听舒翁的感情状况吗？
t_en: Heh, trying to peer into Siobhan's love life, huh?

t_ch: 舒翁这么有魅力，性格也温柔，想和她约会的人可多了，人家当然有兴趣。但邀约过好几次，都被拒绝了，就只能转移目标了呀。
t_en: Siobhan is so attractive and kind, and everyone wants to date her. I was interested too and asked her out a few times, but got rejected. So, I had to find someone else.

t_en: Well, it seems you enjoy discussing embarrassing topics. It's making me a bit flustered, to be honest.

t_ch: 请再给我杯饮料降降温吧，这次想要<color=#dbc291ff>梦幻风格，超级浓郁的类型！还是要多放点冰块，</color>不然人家的电路板要烧坏啦。
t_en: Give me another drink to cool me down some more. This time, I want something <color=#dbc291ff>dreamy and super thick! And like before, I want more ice,</color> or my circuits are gonna fry.

t_ch: （…是不是直接把冰块敷在头上会更有效果？）
t_en: (...Would it be more effective if I just place ice cubes on her head?)

t_ch: 您真是个完美的调饮师！技艺如此高超，又如此地懂得人家的心。体验实在是太棒了。
t_en: You're a perfect drinksmith, so skilled and caring! This was a great experience.

t_ch: 和您聊天的感觉真畅快。在人家看来，您作为调饮师的魅力不会输舒翁太多，很厉害啦。
t_en: It was nice talking to you. In my opinion, you're almost as great a drinksmith as Siobhan. Impressive, indeed.

t_ch: 下次还会来找您的，要对人家的到来充满期待呀！
t_en: I'll drop by next time! Please look forward to my arrival!

t_ch: 在关键时候犯错可是会很掉好感度的，别白白浪费了人家给您的机会呀。
t_en: Making a mistake at a critical time will lose you my favor. Please don't waste the opportunity presented to you.

t_ch: 和您聊天的感觉真畅快。在人家看来，您作为调饮师的魅力不会输舒翁太多，很厉害啦。
t_en: It was nice talking to you. In my opinion, you're almost as great a drinksmith as Siobhan. Impressive, indeed.

t_ch: 下次还会来找您的，要对人家的到来充满期待呀！
t_en: I'll drop by next time! Please look forward to my arrival!
...Hey.

t_ch: 来，靠近点。偷偷问你，刚才是不是有个红色的客人来过？
t_en: Hey, come closer. Let's keep this just between us... Did a customer in red come just now?

t_ch: 我听不清！
t_en: What did you say? I can't hear you!

t_ch: 小、小点声！闹这么大动静，让它发现了怎么办！
t_en: H—hey! Keep your voice down! What if she hears it!?

t_ch: 它刚才也问过类似的问题。
t_en: She asked a similar question just now.
...Huh!? Oh... I see...

t_ch: 我跟着它走到这家酒吧门口，又亲眼看着它从里面出来，所以别想蒙混过关。它都跟你聊了些什么，有没有提到我的名字？
t_en: I followed her right to the door of this lounge and watched her come out, so don't try to fool me. What did she talk to you about? And did she mention my name?

t_ch: 它提到了那个忧郁的客人。
t_en: She mentioned a melancholic customer.

t_ch: 我就知道，我就知道！它还是对那个该死的诗人念念不忘！恶心的文艺腔就这么迷人，让它一直牵挂到现在？
t_en: I knew it, I knew it! She just can't get enough of that blasted poet! Is his pretentious literary accent so fascinating that she's still holding onto it?

t_ch: 它提到了舒翁的名字。
t_en: She mentioned Siobhan.

t_ch: 正常。在这的怪物就没有不喜欢舒翁的，当然不包括我。哼，我根本不怕她。
t_en: That's typical. All the monsters here are fond of Siobhan, but except for me, of course! Hmph, I'm not afraid of her at all.

t_ch: …除了舒翁外，它还有没有提到其他名字…我是说，怪物？
t_en: ...Did she mention anyone else...? I mean, any other monsters?

t_ch: 它提到了那个忧郁的客人。
t_en: She mentioned a melancholic customer.

t_ch: 我就知道，我就知道！它还是对那个该死的诗人念念不忘！恶心的文艺腔就这么迷人，让它一直牵挂到现在？
t_en: I knew it, I knew it! She just can't get enough of that blasted poet! Is his pretentious literary accent so fascinating that she's still holding onto it?

t_ch: 罢了。为那种无聊的事生气，不值得！做好你的本职工作，给我来杯饮料。
t_en: Well, forget it. It's not worth getting worked up about such nonsense! Just do your job and get me a drink.

t_ch: 要<color=#dbc291ff>大杯的，有点苦味的，颜色好看的</color>。你懂什么叫好看吗？就是<color=#dbc291ff>层次给我分的清楚点</color>，别浑浊得像杯脏水！
t_en: I want something in a <color=#dbc291ff>large glass that is a little bitter and with pretty colors</color>. You know what "pretty" means? It means to <color=#dbc291ff>separate the layers more clearly</color>. Don't make it all cloudy like dishwashing water!

t_ch: （…它还是这么坚持要颜色分层啊。）
t_en: (...He's still so fixated on layering colors.)

t_ch: 哈！——这还差不多，能让我满意的人没几个，我算是对你稍微有点好印象了。
t_en: Ha! That's more like it! Not many people can satisfy me. Now, I'm slightly impressed with you.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，又想起来个事。上次我来店里的时候，你是不是还对我无礼来着？
t_en: Oh, hey, I just remembered something. Were you rude to me the last time I came here?

t_ch: 我是不是…说过要教训教训你？
t_en: And did I say... I'd teach you a lesson?

t_ch: 没有，是你记错了。
t_en: No, you remembered it wrong.

t_ch: （准备掏出球棒。）
t_en: (Get ready to pull out your baseball bat.)

t_ch: 别激动。我回去后仔细想了想，那时候是我不对，说话冲了点，产生了些误会哈。
t_en: Hey, chill out. I thought about it, and I realized I was a bit too aggressive, and there might be some kind of misunderstanding.

t_ch: 我向你道歉，希望修复我们之间的关系。所以你看，下次那位红色客人来的时候，能不能帮我说点好话？不用夸奖，多提提我的名字就好！
t_en: I apologize for that, and I hope we can be on better terms. So you see, next time that guest comes around, could you put in a good word for me? No need for flattery. Just mention my name more often!

t_ch: 这点小要求，你应该不会拒绝吧？
t_en: You'll do me this small favor, won't you?

t_ch: 得看我的心情。
t_en: Well, it depends on my mood.

t_ch: 想清楚点，这件事对你也有好处。你要是让我开心，我就会常来光顾这家酒吧，你也能拿到更多小费。
t_en: Think about it: It's beneficial for you too. If you make me happy, I'll drop by more often, and you'll make more tips.

t_ch: 对我有好处吗？
t_en: What's in it for me?

t_ch: 当然有。你要是让我开心，我就会常来光顾这家酒吧，你也能拿到更多小费。
t_en: You see, if you make me happy, I'll drop by more often, and you'll receive more tips.

t_ch: 很棒的提议，对吧？你收钱，我享受服务，双方都有得赚。
t_en: A great deal, isn't it? You get paid, and I get served. A win-win situation for both of us.

t_ch: 在这个破烂的酒吧里，谁都得听我的，整天调饮料有什么前途，要是选择站在我这边，我保你后面能过得顺心！
t_en: Everyone in this lousy lounge takes orders from me! What's the point of mixing drinks all the time? Stay on my side, and I'll ensure you a happy life!

t_ch: 但是我拒绝。
t_en: I refuse.

t_ch: 我的想法是…
t_en: My idea is...

t_ch: 没有人能拒绝我的建议。因为我总是对的。
t_en: Nobody refuses my offers, because I'm always right.

t_ch: 但是我拒绝。
t_en: I refuse.

t_ch: <size=28>…你！不知好歹！</size>
t_en: <size=28>...You! You have no idea what's good for you!</size>

t_ch: 我就不该对个身份低微的服务员抱什么期待！调杯能喝的饮料是他们最大的作用了！
t_en: I shouldn't have expected anything from a lowly waiter! Making drinks is the only thing you're good for!

t_ch: 给我来个<color=#dbc291ff>超大杯、特别甜、味道特别刺激的饮料，还得有漂亮的分层！</color>要是哪点没达到要求，我都不会付钱！
t_en: Give me something in an <color=#dbc291ff>extra large glass, very sweet, and tastes super intense. And it's gotta have pretty layers!</color> I'm not paying if any of my criteria are not met!

t_ch: （要是敢不付钱，你觉得我会让你离开吗？）
t_en: (Do you think I'd let you leave without paying?)

t_ch: 你自己看看，这种东西能给客人喝吗？！需求讲得明明白白，照着做都不会？
t_en: Look! How can you serve this to your customers!? I've told you what I need, and you can't even do what you're told!?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，又想起来个事。上次我来店里的时候，你是不是还对我无礼来着？
t_en: Oh, hey, I just remembered something. Were you rude to me the last time I came here?

t_ch: 我是不是…说过要教训教训你？
t_en: And did I say... I'd teach you a lesson?

t_ch: 没有，是你记错了。
t_en: No, you remembered it wrong.

t_ch: （准备掏出球棒。）
t_en: (Get ready to pull out your baseball bat.)

t_ch: 别激动。我回去后仔细想了想，那时候是我不对，说话冲了点，产生了些误会哈。
t_en: Hey, chill out. I thought about it, and I realized I was a bit too aggressive, and there might be some kind of misunderstanding.

t_ch: 我向你道歉，希望修复我们之间的关系。所以你看，下次那位红色客人来的时候，能不能帮我说点好话？不用夸奖，多提提我的名字就好！
t_en: I apologize for that, and I hope we can be on better terms. So you see, next time that guest comes around, could you put in a good word for me? No need for flattery. Just mention my name more often!

t_ch: 这点小要求，你应该不会拒绝吧？
t_en: You'll do me this small favor, won't you?

t_ch: 得看我的心情。
t_en: Well, it depends on my mood.

t_ch: 想清楚点，这件事对你也有好处。你要是让我开心，我就会常来光顾这家酒吧，你也能拿到更多小费。
t_en: Think about it: It's beneficial for you too. If you make me happy, I'll drop by more often, and you'll make more tips.

t_ch: 对我有好处吗？
t_en: What's in it for me?

t_ch: 当然有。你要是让我开心，我就会常来光顾这家酒吧，你也能拿到更多小费。
t_en: You see, if you make me happy, I'll drop by more often, and you'll receive more tips.

t_ch: 很棒的提议，对吧？你收钱，我享受服务，双方都有得赚。
t_en: A great deal, isn't it? You get paid, and I get served. A win-win situation for both of us.

t_ch: 在这个破烂的酒吧里，谁都得听我的，整天调饮料有什么前途，要是选择站在我这边，我保你后面能过得顺心！

t_ch: （要是敢不付钱，你觉得我会让你离开吗？）
t_en: (Do you think I'd let you leave without paying?)

t_ch: 哼。有点本事啊，别以为这样就结束了，直到我离开这家店为止，你都得为我服务。
t_en: Hmph, not bad, but this is not the end. You'll need to serve me until I leave this lounge.

t_ch: 呼…消消气。喝得差不多，但我要说的还没完。你打算在这家酒吧工作多久？到底还要过多长时间我才会见不到你？
t_en: Well... I've calmed down a bit. I've almost finished my drink, but I'm not done talking. How long are you intending to work at this lounge? How long do I have to wait if I don't want to see your face again?

t_ch: 直到倒闭的那天吧。
t_en: Until the day this lounge shuts down.

t_ch: 你来，我就在。
t_en: Whenever you come, I'll be here.

t_ch: 想到要和你打交道就恼火。啧，我就勉为其难地教教你，作为服务员的自我修养！
t_en: The very thought of talking to you annoys me. Tsk, fine! I'll show you how to be a competent attendant!

t_ch: 结束后来旁边找我！我在这里等着！
t_en: Meet me there when you're done! I'll be waiting!

t_ch: 哈哈哈哈，我说吧，稍微难点的事就做不好，你也就这点能耐了。
t_en: Hahahaha, just as I thought! You can't handle anything slightly challenging! That's all you're capable of.

t_ch: 我还是会掏钱，但要记住——这是我赏给你的！
t_en: I'll still pay for it, but remember — it's my treat!

t_ch: 呼…消消气。喝得差不多，但我要说的还没完。你打算在这家酒吧工作多久？到底还要过多长时间我才会见不到你？
t_en: Well... I've calmed down a bit. I've almost finished my drink, but I'm not done talking. How long are you intending to work at this lounge? How long do I have to wait if I don't want to see your face again?

t_ch: 直到倒闭的那天吧。
t_en: Until the day this lounge shuts down.

t_ch: 你来，我就在。
t_en: Whenever you come, I'll be here.

t_ch: 想到要和你打交道就恼火。啧，我就勉为其难地教教你，作为服务员的自我修养！
t_en: The very thought of talking to you annoys me. Tsk, fine! I'll show you how to be a competent attendant!

t_ch: 结束后来旁边找我！我在这里等着！
t_en: Meet me there when you're done! I'll be waiting!

t_ch: 先转会搅拌棒。
t_en: Move the mixer first.

t_ch: 哼，尽是些华而不实的玩意。看着漂亮，作用还真没有！
t_en: Hmph, nothing but fluff and flash. Pretty on the outside, useless otherwise!

t_ch: （这话轮得到你来说吗？）
t_en: (I don't think you're in a position to say this?)

t_ch: （它对我的态度还是这么蛮横啊。）
t_en: (It's still being so rude to me.)

t_ch: （不过从聊天里知道了不少铁皮人的事，它应该愿意跟我说说心里话了吧？）
t_en: (But I learnt a lot about Tin Man through our chat. It should be willing to be honest with me now.)

t_ch: 记录「铁皮人」的情况。
t_en: Record information about the Tin Man.

t_ch: 你知道吗，我们是没法相互理解的。再怎么努力交流，终究会形同陌路。
t_en: You know, we can't understand each other. Despite our efforts to communicate, our paths are destined to remain apart.

t_ch: 那言语的价值是什么？我们拼命发出自己的声音，只是徒劳吗？
t_en: If that's the case, what purpose does language serve? Is our desperate cry for understanding merely an exercise in futility?

t_ch: 陌生的调饮师，你能给我答案吗？
t_en: Tell me, strange drinksmith. Do you hold an answer?

t_ch: 不能，少问。
t_en: No, I don't. And stop asking.

t_ch: 唉，明知那扇门已经对我紧紧闭上，却还总是忍不住地去敲。
t_en: *Sigh* I can't help but knock on the door I know is closed to me.

t_ch: 很多事本来就是徒劳无功的。
t_en: Many things are doomed to futility.

t_ch: 唉，追寻这种问题的答案，就像在车站等一艘不会到来的船，永远得不到结果。
t_en: *sigh* Seeking answers to such questions is akin to waiting at a station for a ship that will never arrive.

t_ch: 算了，没什么好说的，还是喝饮料吧。只有那温润的液体，能给予我痛苦的心灵以些许慰藉。
t_en: Let it go. Let's simply have a drink. Only the warmth of that liquid can offer solace to my tormented soul.

t_ch: 请给我<color=#dbc291ff>小杯装的眼泪，此刻我只想安静品尝生活带给我的强烈苦涩。</color>
t_en: Please give me a <color=#dbc291ff>small glass of tears. I just wish to quietly savor the intense bitterness that life has brought me</color>.

t_ch: 要是你手头碰巧…我是说碰巧，有什么饰品，<color=#dbc291ff>能让我回想起剧院里的那些观众，</color>就放上去吧，我想看。谢谢。
t_en: If you happen to... and I do mean happen to have some decorations <color=#dbc291ff>that can remind me of the audience back at the theater</color>, please put it on. I'd like to see it. Thank you.

t_ch: （能代表匹诺康尼观众的装饰物…我得仔细去挑选下。）
t_en: (A decoration that represents Penacony... I'll have to pick one carefully.)

t_ch: 令人赞叹的杰作！看啊，你的双手是何等地神奇，轻易便能抚平我内心的创伤！
t_en: A wondrous masterpiece! Your hands possess an enchanting touch, effortlessly mending the wounds within me!

t_ch: 唉。热烈的情感转瞬即逝，玻璃杯已然见底，而我内心的空洞仍未填满。
t_en: *sigh* Passionate emotions are transient. The glass is empty, yet the void in my heart remains unfilled.

t_ch: 好在，这杯迷人的饮料让我与你相识了。
t_en: Thankfully, this delightful drink has made us acquaintances.

t_ch: 你知道吗，来到这家酒吧前，我只是个落魄的戏剧演员，扮演过无数不知名的小角色，始终郁郁不得志。没有方向，没有收入，没有自我。
t_en: You know, before I came to this lounge, I was a struggling actor relegated to countless minor roles, plagued by desolation. No direction, no income, and no self-esteem.

t_ch: 我这一生，尽是遗憾，你说我还能获得幸福吗？
t_en: Regrets have permeated my life. Do you think I can still discover happiness?

t_ch: 问就是不能。
t_en: If you have to get an answer, then it's "no."

t_ch: 唉，你道出了生活的真相。提出问题前，询问者的心中通常都已有了答案。
t_en: *Sigh* You've spoken truthfully about life. More often than not, the questioner already holds an answer in mind before asking the question.

t_ch: 所谓的幸福是什么呢？
t_en: What is "happiness" anyway?

t_ch: 唉，你问得好，繁华皆是表象，其后的狼藉与痛楚从不为世人所知晓。
t_en: *sigh* A piercing question, indeed. Happiness is but a facade, with the world oblivious to the pain and suffering concealed beneath.

t_ch: 我就是个活在戏里的角色，从来只会模仿别人的话语，改不掉，也丢不了。
t_en: I'm like a character in a play, confined to mimicking the words of others. I can't change the situation or myself.

t_ch: 过去不断追赶着我，可怖的梦魇始终萦绕着我，尽管找到了这处乐园，我心中的悲伤也从来没有减少分毫。那不是简单几杯饮料就能消除的痛楚，而是……
t_en: The past continues to haunt me. Even in this paradise, the dreadful nightmare persists, and the sadness within me never fades. It's not something a few drinks can wash away, but...

t_ch: 细说。
t_en: Do go on.

t_ch: 你是喝多了吧？
t_en: Are you drunk already?

t_ch: 唉，未经允许就向旁人揭露自己的伤疤是很失礼的事情。适当地表露悲伤，适当地感到喜悦，适当地接受离别，这对彼此都好。
t_en: *sigh* It's impolite to expose my wounds without permission. It's better to share sorrows, experience joys, and bid farewell appropriately.

t_ch: 还是先点单吧。请给我<color=#dbc291ff>一大杯越浓稠越好的饮料，味道稍微刺激点。最重要的——再来点爱，谢谢。</color>
t_en: Better make an order first. Please give me something in <color=#dbc291ff>a large glass, as thick as you can make it, and tastes a little intense. Most importantly — a little more love, please</color>.

t_ch: …你说的爱是？
t_en: ...What's the "love" you mentioned?

t_ch: 我曾疯狂地迷恋戏剧，那是由缤纷华服与妆饰共同构造起的幻梦。
t_en: Once, I was fervently obsessed with plays, dreams woven from vibrant costumes and make-up.

t_ch: 可惜，我的爱早已遗失在了过去。
t_en: Unfortunately, my love became lost in the past.

t_ch: （…按它的习惯，应该是指放个跟动画角色有关的装饰物上去吧？）
t_en: (...Based on its habits, it probably means it wants a decoration related to cartoon characters?)

t_ch: 这是何等地不幸。世界以痛吻我，还要我付小费！
t_en: How unfortunate. The world kisses me with pain yet expects a tip in return!

t_ch: 唉。热烈的情感转瞬即逝，玻璃杯已然见底，而我内心的空洞仍未填满。

t_en: (...Based on its habits, it probably means it wants a decoration related to cartoon characters?)

t_ch: 时间啊，在此停驻吧！这杯饮料实在太过美妙，我将用一生去回味。
t_en: O, time! Cease your march! This drink is so exquisite, and I yearn to savor its flavor for the remainder of my days.

t_ch: 即使心头被愁云笼罩，你的善意与调饮技巧仍旧给我留下了极深的印象。
t_en: Despite the sorrow clouding my heart, your kindness and drink-mixing mastery have deeply impressed me.

t_ch: 唉，就很郁闷，离别总是让人感伤，但请…不要后悔和我的相遇。
t_en: *sigh* Farewells always carry a tinge of bitterness, but please... do not lament our encounter.

t_ch: 并非是我所点的味道。唉，就连尝到如愿的饮料，对现今的我而言也是遥不可及吗？
t_en: This is not the flavor I ordered. *sigh* Is it too much to even ask for a drink that quenches my thirst?

t_ch: 即使心头被愁云笼罩，你的善意与调饮技巧仍旧给我留下了极深的印象。
t_en: Despite the sorrow clouding my heart, your kindness and drink-mixing mastery have deeply impressed me.

t_ch: 唉，就很郁闷，离别总是让人感伤，但请…不要后悔和我的相遇。
t_en: *sigh* Farewells always carry a tinge of bitterness, but please... do not lament our encounter.

t_ch: …您好？我们又见面啦。
t_en: ...Hello, there? Here we meet again.

t_ch: 那位忧郁的客人刚走。
t_en: That melancholic customer just left.

t_ch: 你也不是来见我的吧？
t_en: You're not here to see me, are you?

t_ch: 嘻嘻，何必这么较真呢。您都做了这么长时间调饮师，应该很清楚，心里话呀，还是不说出来为妙。
t_en: Hee-hee, why so serious? You've been a drinksmith for quite a while, and I'm sure you know better than to speak your mind.

t_ch: 其实，人家过来找您，是听说了件非常有意思的事，想和您分享。
t_en: Well, I came to see you because I heard something interesting and wanted to share it.

t_ch: 要是没兴趣的话，那就算了吧。
t_en: Well, if you're not interested, then forget it.

t_ch: 我最喜欢吃瓜了！
t_en: Oh, I love gossip!

t_ch: 那就算了吧。
t_en: Alright. Forget it then.

t_ch: 真的不想听？人家准备直接回去了哦。
t_en: You really don't want to hear it? Fine. I'll just head home.

t_ch: 我最喜欢吃瓜了！
t_en: Oh, I love gossip!

t_ch: 没什么意思，还是算了吧。
t_en: No, I don't. Let's just forget it.

t_ch: 哎，可人家偏偏想要讲给您听，倾听客人的话不是调饮师的职责嘛，懈怠工作的话，人家要去向舒翁告状了。
t_en: Even so, I still want to tell you. Isn't it part of a drinksmith's job to listen to your customers? If you slack off, I might have to file a complaint with Siobhan.

t_ch: 好吧，都随你。
t_en: Fine, whatever.

t_ch: 保住工作要紧。
t_en: Alright. I don't want to risk losing my job.

t_ch: 嘻嘻，还是上钩了。不过现在有些渴了，烦请您先给人家调杯饮料吧。
t_en: Hee-hee, great! But I'm feeling a bit thirsty now. Could you make me a drink first?

t_ch: 唔，这次想要<color=#dbc291ff>松弛感，清清爽爽的，是我喜欢的类型哦。可别加太多冰，</color>人家最近身体很虚。
t_en: Mm, this time, I want a <color=#dbc291ff>uplifting and refreshing drink that I like. Don't add too much ice</color>. I've been feeling under the weather recently.

t_ch: （记得以前都会要超多冰的，是因为没聊到害羞的话题，不需要降温了嘛？）
t_en: (I remember she used to ask for a lot of ice cubes. So, she doesn't need ice cubes this time because she hasn't brought up any embarrassing topics?)

t_ch: 味道真好，您还是这么懂人家的心。真希望您能永远在这里工作呀，永远都在人家身边。
t_en: This tastes amazing! You still understand me so well. If only you could stay here forever, by my side.

t_ch: 好啦，喝完调制的饮料，也该把那件好玩的事告诉您了，就是呀——
t_en: Well, now that I've finished my drink, it's time to share that interesting story with you...

t_ch: 人家呢，偶然间得知了舒翁的秘密。
t_en: Actually, I stumbled upon Siobhan's secret by chance.

t_ch: …细说？
t_en: ...Care to explain?

t_ch: 是哪种秘密？
t_en: What kind of secret?

t_ch: 嘻嘻，当然是所有人都会感兴趣的那种秘密啦。
t_en: Hee-hee, the kind of secret that would pique anyone's interest, of course.

t_ch: 人家不是打算在梦境酒店里逛逛嘛，就在不久前，来酒吧的路上，碰见了个打扮很漂亮的女孩子，看来看去的不知道在找什么。
t_en: I was wandering around the hotel in the Dreamscape when I spotted a gorgeously dressed lady searching for something.

t_ch: 在这里遇见外面的客人可是稀罕事，所以人家就多跟了段时间。然后呀，就听见那个女孩子嘴里念念有词，好像是说——
t_en: It's rare to come across an outsider in this place, so I followed her for a while. Then, I overheard her murmuring something like...

t_ch: 想约舒翁出门去玩，可舒翁拒绝了她好多次，躲在这不肯走，她就直接找上门来啦。
t_en: She wanted to hang out with Siobhan but had been turned down multiple times. So, she decided to come straight to the lounge.

t_ch: 还说什么…现在的舒翁肯定会答应她，感觉像是认识了很长时间呢。
t_en: She even said... Siobhan would definitely accept her offer this time... It's as if they've known each other for ages.

t_ch: 假设您就是这位当事人的话，还会继续联系对方嘛？
t_en: If you were in that lady's shoes, would you keep trying to contact Siobhan?

t_ch: 不达目的绝不罢休！
t_en: I wouldn't give up until I got what I wanted!

t_ch: 就喜欢您这样的气势！人家也要朝这个方向继续努力！
t_en: That's what I like about you! I should have the same attitude!

t_ch: 感谢你的特别邀请。
t_en: Thank you for your invitation.

t_ch: 呀！听着就很难受。虽然您这么说…可心里头的想法哪有这么容易压下去呀。
t_en: Ohh, that's tough. Even if you say that... it's not easy to suppress what's on your mind.

t_ch: …跟您说这个，是因为人家也藏着不少秘密，犹豫要不要讲出来。
t_en: ...I'm sharing this with you because I'm also holding onto many secrets, and I'm hesitant to reveal them.

t_ch: 但又是胆怯，又是难为情的，很多话到嘴边，好几次都硬生生咽回去了。唉。
t_en: But then again, I'm too timid, and those words get stuck in my throat, and swallowed back. *sigh*

t_ch: 到底要不要把那位约出来，大胆表明自己的心意呢……
t_en: Should I ask him out and boldly express my feelings to him...

t_ch: 该乘胜追击！
t_en: Seize the chance while you have it!

t_ch: 春宵苦短啊！
t_en: Don't make decisions you'll regret!

t_ch: 唔，哪有这么简单……
t_en: Hmm, but it's not that simple...

t_ch: 还是麻烦您调杯饮料吧，想要<color=#dbc291ff>小杯，微微苦的，味道要特别特别柔和，稍微放些冰块就好。</color>就像人家现在的心情，烦闷呀。
t_en: I'd like to bother you to make a drink that is <color=#dbc291ff>very slightly bitter in a small glass, and it has to taste really mellow. Just a little ice will do</color>. Just like my current mood: Upset.

t_ch: （…它在烦恼的，应该是和忧郁派相关的事吧？）
t_en: (...Is she worrying about something related to Melancholy?)

t_ch: 是手抖放错材料了吗？按我对您技术的了解，不应该会出这么明显的纰漏呀。
t_en: Did you accidentally use the wrong ingredients? I didn't expect you to make such an obvious mistake.

t_ch: 好啦，喝完调制的饮料，也该把那件好玩的事告诉您了，就是呀——
